plan: Fix uncoalesced global memory access in histogram_smem_atomics by swapping the loop order so threads walk across columns (coalesced) in the inner loop. Currently the kernel iterates 'col' in the outer loop and 'row' in the inner loop, meaning consecutive threads (differing in threadIdx.x) access pixels separated by 'width' elements in memory (in[row * width + col]). Swap to: outer loop over row (stride ny), inner loop over col (stride nx), so that when the warp reads 'in[row * width + col]' the 32 threads in a warp hit consecutive addresses (col, col+1, ..., col+31). This directly addresses the 57% global load efficiency. Apply the same fix to histogram_gmem_atomics. Be careful: the iteration range and strides (nx, ny) remain the same; only the loop nesting changes. Correctness risk: none if strides are kept. Performance risk: none; this is a pure latency/bandwidth improvement.

Increase occupancy by reducing register pressure in histogram_smem_atomics. The kernel uses 30 registers/thread on sm_60, limiting warps/SM. Add '__launch_bounds__(128, 4)' (or '__launch_bounds__(128, 6)') to the histogram_smem_atomics kernel to hint the compiler to spill registers to achieve the target active blocks. Also consider replacing the per-channel local array 'unsigned int bins[ACTIVE_CHANNELS]' with direct inline computation at the atomicAdd call site to remove register live-ranges. Target occupancy above 32% (from the current 16.5%). Correctness risk: register spills increase latency per instruction slightly, but the 87.2% memory stall rate means the GPU has ample latency-hiding capacity if more warps are resident. Risk: if spill traffic exceeds smem/L1 bandwidth, throughput can regress; validate timing before committing.

Increase grid size to better saturate the GPU. Currently the kernel launches a fixed 16x16=256 blocks regardless of image size or SM count. Query cudaDeviceProp at launch to get multiProcessorCount, then set grid so total_blocks = multiProcessorCount * 8 (or similar multiple). For a 1920x1080 image with 2 million pixels, 256 blocks of 128 threads each means each block processes ~8000 pixels; a larger grid (e.g., 64x64=4096 blocks) would reduce per-block work and improve tail latency while keeping the smem footprint (3084 bytes per block) well within limits. The partial histogram buffer size (total_blocks * NUM_PARTS * 4 bytes) grows proportionally: ensure it stays within device memory. The second-pass accumulation kernel already handles variable 'n', so it remains correct. Performance risk: too many blocks may cause cudaMalloc overhead or exhaust device memory for large NUM_PARTS; cap total_blocks at a reasonable bound (e.g., 4096).

Eliminate the cudaMalloc/cudaFree of d_part_hist inside the timed region by pre-allocating it once outside the timing loop. In run_smem_atomics (and run_gmem_atomics), the partial histogram buffer is allocated and freed every call. For the benchmark's 100-iteration timing loop this adds cudaMalloc latency inside each measured call. Refactor run_smem_atomics to accept a pre-allocated d_part_hist pointer, or allocate it once before the timing loop in the caller (TestMethods / RunTest). The GpuTimer wraps both kernel launches but also the cudaMalloc call implicitly synchronizes, inflating measured time. Move the allocation outside the timer. Correctness risk: none if the buffer is zeroed properly before each kernel launch (the smem kernel zeros smem at startup, so the partial buffer only needs to be sized correctly). Performance risk: none; this is an overhead reduction.

Use 16-bit (uint16) privatized counters in shared memory for the first-pass smem_atomics kernel to halve smem usage and improve occupancy. With NUM_BINS=256 and ACTIVE_CHANNELS=3, the current smem array is 3*256*4=3072 bytes per block. Switching to uint16_t reduces it to 1536 bytes, allowing more blocks to be resident per SM. Each thread block processes at most (width*height)/(total_blocks) pixels; for a 1920x1080 image with 256 blocks that is ~8100 pixels per block, well below the uint16 overflow threshold of 65535. Change 'unsigned int smem[...]' to 'unsigned short smem[...]', use atomicAdd on unsigned short (supported on sm_60+ via the 16-bit atomicAdd intrinsic), and widen to 32-bit before writing to the global partial histogram in the store loop. Correctness risk: overflow if any bin receives more than 65535 increments within a single block; verify that max pixels/block < 65535 before enabling. If the grid is made larger (plan 3), per-block counts decrease, making overflow even less likely.
